title: Peppery Filipino Tapioca Crumble
description: A decadent and indulgent dessert featuring caramelized sugar sauce with a peppery twist, served as a dessert.
instructions: Rinse the tapioca pearls in cold water, and soak them in hot water for at least an hour.
Drain the soaked pearls and rinse again. Heat a bit of vegetable oil in a pot over medium heat.
Add the pearl to the pot, reduce heat to low, and cook until they are soft but still hold their shape (about 5-7 minutes).
Meanwhile, combine coconut sugar with ground black pepper and salt in a small bowl. Set aside for later.
After pearls are cooked, remove them from heat, drain, and stir in the lemon juice.
Pour the pearl mixture into a shallow dish or mold to cool and set (this step is optional but recommended).
Once cooled, cut the cooled pearls into small pieces and place on serving plates. Drizzle with the caramel sauce made by heating up the sugar mixture until it turns amber.
